question.text: A trader buys a call option on Infosys with strike Rs. 1,500 at a premium of Rs. 45. At expiry, Infosys is at Rs. 1,530. What is the profit/loss per share?

explanation.text:
**The Logic:** Call Profit = max(S - X, 0) - Premium
Step-by-step: 1. S = 1,530, X = 1,500, P = 45 2. Payoff = max(1,530 - 1,500, 0) = Rs. 30 3. Profit = 30 - 45 = -Rs. 15 (LOSS) 4. BEP = 1,500 + 45 = 1,545. Stock at 1,530 < 1,545, so still in loss.
**The Trap:** Even though the option is ITM (S > X), the buyer has a net LOSS because the payoff doesn't cover the premium.
**Cross-Exam Context:** ITM at expiry does NOT guarantee profit. Profit only when S > BEP = X + P.
**Key Takeaway:** ITM but below BEP = partial loss. Profit only when Spot > Strike + Premium.
